skill.name: object-store-best-practices
skill.description: Guides reviews of Rust object_store usage for reliable S3, Azure Blob, and Google Cloud Storage operations.
skill.summary: Review Rust object_store code for retries, streaming, error handling, and efficient cloud storage patterns.
skill.author: EmilLindfors

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, filesystem, sensitive-storage, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found they are Markdown examples and prose in a single SKILL.md file. The skill only declares Read and Grep tools and does not include executable scripts, network calls, prompt-injection text, or data exfiltration logic. Residual risk is low because the skill discusses cloud credentials and storage operations, so generated advice should still be reviewed before production use.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The repeated Ruby or shell backtick findings are false positives. The backticks delimit inline Markdown terms and Rust code examples, not shell execution performed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown inline code or fenced Rust examples. No executable Ruby, shell script, or command invocation exists in the skill package.

low_findings.title: Asynchronous Task Example Misclassified as Process Spawn
low_findings.description: The high spawn finding is a false positive. The cited line shows tokio::spawn in an illustrative Rust snippet about reusing an ObjectStore, not OS process creation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding code is a Markdown suggestion block for Rust async tasks. It does not call child_process, std::process, shell commands, or external binaries.

low_findings.title: Filesystem and Browser Storage Findings Are Benign Examples
low_findings.description: The tempfile and browser-storage findings are false positives. The tempfile line appears in a testing best-practice example, and the local storage text refers to a local filesystem test backend.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both lines are inside a Rust test example that uses a temporary directory for local tests. There is no browser API access or skill-level file write behavior.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance and Weak-Crypto Findings Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and network reconnaissance terms, but context shows best-practice prose and Rust storage examples. No evidence found of host probing, network scanning, encryption downgrade, or cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are normal documentation about object storage, streaming, metadata HEAD requests, and reliability. No scanning command, cryptographic primitive, or malicious network target is present.

content.value_statement: Cloud object storage code often fails under transient errors, large files, and expensive listing patterns. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code review Rust object_store usage for production-ready reliability.
actual_capabilities: Identifies missing retry configuration for object_store clients.
Reviews error handling for unwrap calls and missing context.
Suggests streaming reads for large objects to reduce memory use.
Recommends multipart upload patterns for large file writes.
Checks listing logic for prefixes, filtering, and cost control.
Highlights safer credential configuration through environment and cloud identity sources.
limitations: Does not execute tests or verify cloud credentials.
Focuses on Rust object_store patterns, not every storage SDK.
Provides review guidance, not a complete architecture assessment.
Cannot confirm provider-specific limits without current project context.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Data Pipeline for Production
use_cases.description: Review object_store reads and writes before deploying a Rust data pipeline that handles large cloud objects.

use_cases.title: Audit Storage Reliability Issues
use_cases.description: Find missing retries, weak timeout settings, and memory-heavy reads that cause intermittent failures.

use_cases.title: Improve Cloud Storage Cost Control
use_cases.description: Check listing and metadata access patterns to reduce unnecessary object store requests.

prompt_templates.title: Check Basic Usage
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small object_store snippet.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Rust object_store code for basic reliability issues. Focus on retries, errors, and obvious memory risks.

prompt_templates.title: Review Large File Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review for upload and download paths.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this object_store upload and download flow. Recommend streaming, multipart upload, timeout, and error handling improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Production Pipeline
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review for a production data workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Rust data pipeline that uses object_store. Prioritize failure modes, cloud cost, concurrency, and observability gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Design Storage Guidelines
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced team guidance for shared storage code.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create review guidance for our object_store abstraction. Include retry policy, credential handling, large object patterns, and test strategy.

output_examples.input: Review code that downloads a large parquet file with result.bytes().await.
output_examples.output: The review flags full-file buffering as a memory risk and recommends stream processing for large objects.

output_examples.input: Assess an S3 client builder that uses default retry settings.
output_examples.output: The review recommends explicit retry configuration, longer timeouts for large files, and clearer error context.

output_examples.input: Inspect code that lists an entire bucket before filtering paths.
output_examples.output: The review highlights request cost and latency risks, then recommends prefix-based listing and server-side narrowing where available.

best_practices: Use explicit retries and timeouts for production object store clients.
Stream large reads and use multipart uploads for large writes.
Use prefixes, filtering, and metadata caching to control request cost.
anti_patterns: Calling unwrap or expect on cloud storage operations.
Loading large objects fully into memory without a size check.
Hardcoding cloud credentials, regions, or bucket names in source code.

faq.question: Which object stores does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It focuses on Rust object_store usage with S3, Azure Blob Storage, and Google Cloud Storage.

faq.question: Does it run cloud operations?
faq.answer: No. It provides review guidance and code suggestions. It does not access cloud accounts.

faq.question: Can it replace provider documentation?
faq.answer: No. Use it for implementation review, then confirm provider-specific behavior against official documentation.

faq.question: Does it help with performance?
faq.answer: Yes. It reviews streaming, multipart upload, connection reuse, concurrency, and listing patterns.

faq.question: Is it useful for tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It suggests LocalFileSystem and mock-based testing patterns for object_store code.

faq.question: What tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
